phases.name: Utility Functions & Color Constants
phases.description: Add shared utility functions (toMinutes) and color constants (CORES_CONTRATO) that TimelineGrid will depend on. These are small additions to existing files.

subtasks.description: Add toMinutes() utility function to formatadores.ts. This function converts a time string 'HH:MM' to total minutes (e.g., '08:00' -> 480, '14:30' -> 870). Add it AFTER the existing iniciais() function. Signature: export function toMinutes(time: string): number. Implementation: split by ':', parseInt hours * 60 + parseInt minutes. Handle null/empty by returning 0.

Add CORES_CONTRATO constant to cores.ts. This maps tipo_contrato names to Tailwind color classes for timeline bars. Add it AFTER the existing CORES_GENERO export. Structure: export const CORES_CONTRATO: Record<string, { bar: string; text: string; border: string }> with these entries: 'CLT 44h' -> emerald (bar: 'bg-emerald-500/80 dark:bg-emerald-600/70', text: 'text-white dark:text-emerald-100', border: 'border-emerald-600 dark:border-emerald-500'), 'CLT 36h' -> blue (bar: 'bg-blue-500/80 dark:bg-blue-600/70', text: 'text-white dark:text-blue-100', border: 'border-blue-600 dark:border-blue-500'), 'Estagiario 20h' -> purple (bar: 'bg-purple-500/80 dark:bg-purple-600/70', text: 'text-white dark:text-purple-100', border: 'border-purple-600 dark:border-purple-500'). Also add a DEFAULT key with slate colors for unknown contract types: bar: 'bg-slate-500/80 dark:bg-slate-600/70', text: 'text-white dark:text-slate-100', border: 'border-slate-600 dark:border-slate-500'. Each entry MUST have dark: variants for dark mode.

phases.name: EscalaViewToggle Component
phases.description: Create a dedicated EscalaViewToggle component for switching between 'grid' and 'timeline' views, with localStorage persistence. The existing ViewToggle.tsx uses 'card' | 'table' modes for list pages -- we need a new one for the escala page with different icons and labels.

subtasks.description: Create EscalaViewToggle.tsx in src/renderer/src/componentes/. This is a NEW file. It should export: (1) type EscalaViewMode = 'grid' | 'timeline', (2) EscalaViewToggle component, (3) useEscalaViewMode hook. Pattern: COPY the exact structure from ViewToggle.tsx (same file) but change: ViewMode -> EscalaViewMode, 'card'|'table' -> 'grid'|'timeline'. Icons: use Table2 (from lucide-react) for 'grid' mode and GanttChart (from lucide-react) for 'timeline' mode. Tooltip labels: 'Grade' for grid, 'Timeline' for timeline. The useEscalaViewMode hook should use localStorage key 'ef-view-escala' and default to 'grid'. The hook must validate stored value is 'grid' or 'timeline' before using it (same pattern as useViewMode). Keep the same Button variant='secondary'|'ghost' toggle pattern. Same size-8 buttons, same rounded corners, same border container div.

phases.name: TimelineGrid Component
phases.description: Create the main TimelineGrid component -- the core of this feature. A CSS Grid based horizontal timeline showing employee shift bars positioned by time along the X axis.

phases.name: Integration into EscalaPagina
phases.description: Wire TimelineGrid and EscalaViewToggle into EscalaPagina.tsx so the user can toggle between grid and timeline views. The toggle must work in all 3 tabs (Simulacao, Oficial, Historico).

phases.name: Polish & Build Verification
phases.description: Verify the full build works, dark mode is consistent, and add any missing details.

subtasks.description: Run full build verification. Execute: (1) npx tsc --noEmit to verify zero TypeScript errors across the entire project. (2) npm run build to verify Electron build completes without errors. Fix any issues found. Common issues to check: (a) Missing imports in TimelineGrid.tsx (React must be imported if using React.Fragment). In modern React 19 with JSX transform, React import is automatic but React.Fragment usage may need explicit import -- use <></> (fragment shorthand) instead to avoid this. (b) CORES_CONTRATO key lookup -- make sure the Record<string, ...> type matches usage. (c) Verify all Tailwind dark: classes are correct syntax. (d) Check that import paths use '@/' alias correctly (e.g., '@/componentes/TimelineGrid', '@/componentes/EscalaViewToggle'). If build fails, read error output and fix the specific issues in the affected files.

verification.command: cd /Users/marcofernandes/escalaflow && npx tsc --noEmit && npm run build
verification.expected: Both commands complete with exit code 0, no errors
